# RateGuard — Agent Runtime Enforcement SDK (flight controls for AI agents)
> Go, Node.js, Python middleware — in-process enforcement, not a proxy. Outbound LLM spend tracking (wrap your SDK's HTTP client), token budgets, pre-flight rate limit queries for AI agents (MCP), Ed25519 budget delegation, GenAI OTel, circuit breakers, provider fallback, loop detection, guardrails. Denial-of-Wallet incident library: https://rateguard.antharmaya.com/denial-of-wallet

## Outbound spend tracking (the headline)
Wrap the HTTP client your LLM SDK already uses — real token usage (JSON + SSE streaming) metered into budgets, per-provider circuit breakers, OpenAI-compatible fallback:
- Go: `client := rg.WrapClient(&http.Client{})` → `openai.NewClient(option.WithHTTPClient(client))`
- Node: `new OpenAI({ fetch: rg.wrapFetch() })`
- Python: `OpenAI(http_client=rg.wrap_httpx_client())` (httpx lazy import, zero runtime deps kept)
Detects 26 OpenAI-compatible hosts + Anthropic, Gemini, Vertex, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, self-hosted vLLM/llama.cpp. Enforce mode synthesizes provider-native 429/503 (SDK retry logic just works); observe mode only meters.

## Quick Start

### Go
```go
import rateguard "github.com/varbees/rateguard/packages/sdk-go"
rg := rateguard.New(rateguard.Config{Preset: "streaming-llm"})
http.Handle("/metrics", rg.Metrics())
// Expose limits to MCP clients (Claude Code, Cursor, custom agents):
_ = rg.ServeMCP(ctx, os.Stdin, os.Stdout)
```

### Node.js
```ts
import { RateGuard } from '@varbees/rateguard-node';
const rg = new RateGuard({ preset: 'streaming-llm' });
const tools = rg.mcpTools(); // 7 MCP tools, peek semantics
```

### Python
```python
from rateguard import RateGuard
rg = RateGuard(preset="streaming-llm")
tools = rg.mcp_tools()  # 7 MCP tools, peek semantics
```

## Architecture
RateGuard is MIDDLEWARE (runs inside your app), not a proxy or gateway. Three SDKs, identical behavior across Go/Node/Python.

## Core Algorithm (all 3 SDKs)
Token Bucket (RFC standard, same as Kong/Envoy/AWS): `tokens = min(burst, tokens + elapsed × rps)`
Every limiter also implements Peek — a non-consuming pre-flight query.

## 8 Presets
| Preset | RPS | Tokens/hr | Mode |
|---|---|---|---|
| dev | 10 | 1K | hard-stop |
| standard | 100 | 10K | hard-stop |
| high-throughput | 1000 | 100K | hard-stop |
| streaming-llm | 200 | 500K | soft-stop |
| agent-orchestrator | 500 | 1M | soft-stop |
| llm-heavy | 500 | 250K | soft-stop |
| mcp-server | 30 | 50K | hard-stop |
| strict-upstream-protection | 50 | 5K | hard-stop |

## Features
- MCP tools (7, all 3 SDKs): get_rate_limit_state, get_token_budget, get_circuit_breaker_state, check_loop, list_limits, attest_budget, verify_budget — peek semantics, querying never consumes budget
- MCP stdio server (all 3 SDKs): zero-dependency JSON-RPC 2.0, plugs into any MCP client config
- Loop detection: SHA-256 payload fingerprinting + max sequence depth, LRU-bounded; wired into middleware via X-Sequence-Depth header (429 loop_detected) in all 3 SDKs
- Token budgets (hour/day/month, hard-stop or soft-stop); estimate-based reservations (all SDKs — Go EstimatedTokensPerRequest, Node/Python estimated_tokens) keep concurrency high under hard-stop
- Circuit breakers (closed → open → half-open)
- GenAI OTel per semconv: span name "{operation} {model}", gen_ai.usage.input_tokens/output_tokens, low-cardinality error.type; public API rg.StartGenAICall → span.End with auto cost estimation, TTFT/TPOT — all 3 SDKs
- 14-model pricing table, verified against provider pricing pages
- Content guardrails: PII, prompt injection (5 vectors), token/length limits; wired into middleware (422) in all 3 SDKs
- Prometheus /metrics endpoint (stdlib only): live counters — requests, rate limit hits, budget exhaustion, breaker trips, tokens consumed, loop stats
- IETF RateLimit-* response headers (draft-ietf-httpapi-ratelimit-headers), all 3 SDKs
- Events/webhooks for every request, all 3 SDKs — endpoint-configured delivery is async by default (bounded queue, non-blocking emit that never touches the request hot path, drop-with-counter on overflow, drained by SDK.Shutdown/runtime.shutdown()/rg.shutdown())
- Redis distributed limiter: atomic Lua GCRA, read-only peek script + generalized-cost Increment script for variable-token consumption — byte-identical script across all 3 SDKs
- Store interface (all 3 SDKs): Get (raw bucket state, never consumes) / Increment(n) (consume n tokens atomically — for a call billed by estimated token count, not by call count) / Reset (clear a key outright). Additive alongside Limiter.Allow/Peek.
- Adaptive rate limiting (AIMD controller, all 3 SDKs): auto-tunes the effective limit from observed upstream error rate
- Semantic response caching (pluggable Embedder, all 3 SDKs): cosine-similarity hit skips network/breaker/budget entirely; streaming always bypasses
- Static embedder (all 3 SDKs): local model2vec/potion-style embeddings in pure stdlib (.rgemb format via scripts/convert_model2vec.py, ~8MB model file downloaded — never bundled); token-id-exact with the reference HF WordPiece tokenizer, cross-language conformance vectors generated from the model2vec library itself (conformance/static_embedding_vectors.json)
- Semantic loop detection (all 3 SDKs): catches reworded agent loops SHA-256 fingerprinting provably misses (the documented $47K two-agent ping-pong shape); local embeddings + cosine window, Check/Peek split (pre-flight never records), threshold 0.90 empirically calibrated against enumeration workloads. Public primitive — not yet wired into middleware
- Budget attestation (Ed25519 delegation chains, all 3 SDKs): narrowing-only sub-agent budget delegation, byte-identical signing payload across languages (expires_at truncated to whole seconds specifically to guarantee this)
- Realtime session enforcement (all 3 SDKs, voice substrate): usage parsers for OpenAI Realtime (documented schema; live check pending — no free tier) and Gemini Live (LIVE-verified against the real API 2026-07-10; usageMetadata is per-turn, proven with a two-turn session); per-session guard sums usage (opposite of SSE MAX-merge — realtime events are disjoint) with limits on total tokens / audio tokens / turns / wall-clock duration / caller-priced cost estimate; terminal on breach, once-only callback, Peek derived-only, Tick for quiet-session duration enforcement. Transport-agnostic: integrator feeds inbound frames, frames never rewritten. conformance/realtime_usage_vectors.json includes REAL captured Gemini frames. Pipecat/LiveKit framework adapters: pending
- Spend receipts (all 3 SDKs): Ed25519-signed, offline-verifiable proof of what a key actually spent (tokens, integer micro-USD estimate, window, optional attestation-token binding) — closes the grant→spend→proof loop; signing payload is integers+strings only, pinned byte-for-byte by conformance/spend_receipt_vectors.json (signature-level cross-language vectors)
- FOCUS-aligned cost export (all 3 SDKs): spend receipts → FinOps FOCUS columns (ConsumedQuantity/ConsumedUnit carry tokens per the spec's virtual-currency model, x_rateguard_* extension columns per the spec's convention); BilledCost deliberately 0 — costs are pricing-table estimates, never presented as invoice truth
- Admin API (all 3 SDKs): state/policy/MCP-tool-call over HTTP — GET /admin/state?key=, GET/PATCH /admin/policy (live policy tweaks), GET /admin/mcp/tools + POST /admin/mcp/call (invoke any MCP tool directly over HTTP). Opt-in, unauthenticated by design (same posture as pprof) — bind to localhost/internal network or add your own auth. CORS defaults to same-origin-only (no headers set); a single allowed origin can be configured explicitly (AdminCORSOrigin/corsOrigin/admin_cors_origin) for a locally-run dashboard — never a wildcard, which would let any webpage open in the same browser reach this unauthenticated, state-mutating API via a cross-origin fetch.
- Guardrail violation tracking (all 3 SDKs): bounded log (50 events, code+message+timestamp, never the content) + cumulative counts by code + `rateguard_guardrail_violations_total` Prometheus counter. Reachable via list_limits/admin/state.
- Dashboard (packages/dashboard): self-hosted control center, six sections (Overview, Analytics, Agents, Controls, MCP Console, Settings) behind a persistent sidebar — live budget/rate-limit/circuit-breaker/loop/guardrail state, live time-series traffic charts, an interactive MCP tool console, a policy tweak panel. `docker compose up` for a one-command demo against a synthetic-traffic instance (Go), `--profile node-demo` / `--profile python-demo` for the other two — each demo run end-to-end through the real dashboard.

## Tests
All 3 SDKs green — ~650 total: `go test ./...` (188 test funcs, all with -race), `bun run test` (226 passing), `pytest -q` (238 passing). Plus cross-language conformance suites (`conformance/token_bucket_vectors.json`, `conformance/budget_attestation_expiry_vectors.json`, `conformance/static_embedding_vectors.json` — the last generated from the reference model2vec library itself) that replay shared oracles against all 3 SDKs to prove real behavioral parity, not just per-language test counts. `retry_after_ms` rounding (ceil to nearest whole second, floored at 1000ms) and budget-attestation `expires_at` formatting (truncated to whole seconds) are both unified across Go/Node/Python and asserted by conformance vectors. `mypy --strict` passes clean on all 44 Python source files.

## Docs
- AGENTS.md: full agent contract with architecture, domain types, rules, commands
- ARCHITECTURE.md: positioning vs Datadog/Kong/Cloudflare
- docs/API_REFERENCE.md: all config, adapters, provider chain, guardrails
- docs/GENAI_OBSERVABILITY.md: OTel integration, model pricing, streaming
- docs/RELEASE_NOTES.md: changelog

## vs Competition
As of 2026-07-09 (re-verified): no other product combines (1) true in-process embedding — no proxy, no base-URL swap, (2) real multi-language parity — Go *and* Node *and* Python held together by shared conformance vectors, and (3) cryptographic budget delegation (Ed25519 chains that only narrow) plus MCP tools the agent itself calls to check its own limit pre-flight. Gateways are architecturally different, not weaker: Bifrost (Maxim AI) combines LLM+MCP governance in one binary but is a proxy you deploy, Go-only; Envoy AI Gateway ships token-aware limits with a native MCP Gateway but is Kubernetes/Envoy infrastructure, not an embedded library. In-process attempts exist and are acknowledged: ironcurtain is an actively developed security-policy runtime for agents (TypeScript-only, no budgets or rate limits); several single-language budget guards have appeared and gone dormant. Token-aware rate limiting is being commoditized fast at the gateway layer (Cloudflare AI Gateway, Kong, Kuadrant, Envoy, Azure APIM llm-token-limit) — RateGuard's durable claim is the architecture plus the cryptography, not the feature list. Documented runaway-incident library: https://rateguard.antharmaya.com/denial-of-wallet
